payload.source_text: The pocket money tracker system allows children to claim pocket money tasks as they are completed throughout the week.
Parents can detail tasks that should be completed and the monetary amount earned for that task.
As a task is completed it is linked to the child that completed it.
The children should be able to see the available tasks and the tasks they have completed that week.
At the end of each week the children can see the total amount they have earned that week.
The Parents can also see a report of how much each child has earned and the tasks they completed so that they can provide the pocket money earned to each child.
At the start of the next week all tasks become available again to be claimed.
Historical task data shall be available.
The resulting system shall be able to operate on iOS 16, Android 9 and Windows 11 or later.

payload.completeness_rule: This register SHALL contain the identifiers of ALL Source elements present in the ledger.

original_descriptions: Enable children to claim and track completed pocket money tasks
Provide children visibility into available and completed task inventory
Track monetary rewards for completed tasks
Weekly earned amount aggregation and presentation
Parent earnings and completion aggregated reporting

payload.evaluated_scope: All input material in this project

payload.completeness_rule: This register SHALL contain the identifiers of ALL ZachmanCell elements present in the ledger.

payload.description: Task claiming and completion tracking
payload.justification: Claiming and tracking represent strategic enterprise functions enabling the core pocket money process.

payload.description: Task assignment definition with monetary valuation
payload.justification: Defining compensated assignments is a strategic function performed by the enterprise to establish work opportunities.

payload.description: Task inventory visibility provisioning
payload.justification: Providing visibility into task status represents a strategic function supporting transparency and engagement.

payload.description: Performance reporting and earnings summary presentation for compensation distribution
payload.justification: Displaying earnings summaries is a strategic function enabling financial awareness and motivation. | Generating performance reports represents a strategic function supporting accurate payment decisions.

payload.description: Historical task data access and analysis
payload.justification: Providing access to longitudinal history is a strategic function enabling accountability and trend analysis.

payload.description: Pocket money task
payload.justification: Tasks are fundamental things of strategic importance referenced across multiple signals as the core compensable work unit.

payload.description: Task assignment
payload.justification: Task assignments are distinct strategic entities representing the linkage between tasks, children, and compensation.

payload.description: Weekly earnings summary
payload.justification: Earnings summary is a strategically important aggregated entity enabling compensation visibility.

payload.description: Child performance report
payload.justification: Performance reporting is a strategic entity providing parental oversight and compensation decision support.

payload.description: Longitudinal task history
payload.justification: Task history represents a strategic data entity supporting trend analysis and accountability functions.

payload.description: Weekly task tracking and earnings cycle
payload.justification: Weekly periodicity is explicitly mentioned as a strategic time cycle governing task completion tracking and earnings visibility.

payload.description: Child user
payload.justification: Children are explicitly identified as a primary stakeholder role with distinct goals across claiming, viewing, and tracking tasks.

payload.description: Parent user
payload.justification: Parents are explicitly identified as a stakeholder role with responsibilities for defining tasks and accessing performance data.

payload.description: Enable children to view available tasks and track completed pocket money tasks
payload.justification: SG049 directly expresses a fundamental purpose/goal of the enterprise system at the strategic level. | SG051 captures a purpose statement representing a strategic transparency goal for child stakeholders.

payload.description: Enable parents to define compensated task assignments with monetary values
payload.justification: SG050 articulates parental motivation as a strategic goal driving the enterprise capability.

payload.description: Enable children to view their weekly earnings summary
payload.justification: SG052 identifies a motivational goal supporting child engagement and financial awareness at the strategic level.

payload.description: Enable parents to access child performance reporting for accurate compensation distribution
payload.justification: SG053 establishes a parental need expressed as a strategic goal ensuring accountability and accurate payments.

payload.description: Provide stakeholders longitudinal task history access supporting accountability
payload.justification: SG054 indicates a requirement framed as a strategic goal for historical analysis and accountability purposes.

payload.description: Child claims completed task for monetary reward
payload.justification: Signal SG290 establishes the core business process of children claiming completed tasks for reward tracking.

payload.description: Parent specifies task with monetary value
payload.justification: Signal SG291 defines the parent capability (function) to specify task entities with associated monetary values.

payload.description: Child views available and completed tasks
payload.justification: Signal SG293 defines child actor visibility requirements as a business function for viewing task entities.

payload.description: System calculates weekly earned monetary totals
payload.justification: Signal SG294 establishes weekly aggregation capability as a business function for calculating earnings.

payload.description: Parent views child-task-earnings report
payload.justification: Signal SG295 defines parent reporting capability as a business function displaying relationships for payment fulfillment.

payload.description: Task availability resets at weekly boundaries
payload.justification: Signal SG296 specifies the cyclical business rule governing task availability reset timing.

payload.description: Historical task data must be retained and accessible
payload.justification: Signal SG297 mandates retention and accessibility as a business rule for historical data management.

payload.description: Task with associated monetary value
payload.justification: Signal SG291 explicitly defines parent capability to specify task entities with monetary value attributes, establishing Task as a core business entity.

payload.description: Child entity claiming completed tasks
payload.justification: Signals SG290 and SG292 establish Child as a business entity that claims completed tasks and is linked to task entities.

payload.description: Monetary value assigned to task
payload.justification: Signal SG291 specifies monetary value as an attribute associated with task entities.

payload.description: Task completion status
payload.justification: Signals SG292 and SG293 distinguish between completed and available tasks, indicating completion status as a task attribute.

payload.description: Weekly aggregated earnings total
payload.justification: Signal SG294 establishes weekly earnings totals as an aggregated monetary attribute visible to child actors.

payload.description: Completed task linked to child claimant
payload.justification: Signal SG292 explicitly specifies the linkage relationship between completed task entities and child entities.

payload.description: Child associated with earned monetary amounts
payload.justification: Signal SG295 defines parent reporting displaying child-task-earnings relationships, establishing the association between child and earnings.

payload.description: Historical task data across temporal periods
payload.justification: Signal SG297 mandates retention of historical task data entities, establishing historical task records as a distinct business entity.

payload.description: Weekly temporal period for earnings aggregation
payload.justification: Signal SG294 establishes weekly aggregation as a recurring temporal cycle for earnings calculation.

payload.description: Weekly task availability reset
payload.justification: Signal SG296 specifies the weekly reset as a recurring business event at temporal boundaries.

payload.description: Temporal scope for task visibility
payload.justification: Signal SG293 references temporal scope within which task visibility is defined, indicating a temporal cycle constraint.

payload.description: Child actor claiming tasks and viewing earnings
payload.justification: Signals SG290, SG293, and SG294 consistently reference the child as an actor performing business activities.

payload.description: Parent actor specifying tasks and viewing reports
payload.justification: Signals SG291 and SG295 define the parent as an actor with capabilities to specify tasks and view reports.

payload.description: Track monetary rewards for completed tasks and support payment fulfillment to children
payload.justification: Signal SG290 establishes reward tracking as the core business objective underlying the system concept. | Signal SG295 explicitly states payment fulfillment as the business goal supported by parent reporting capability.

payload.description: Child visibility limited to temporal scope
payload.justification: Signal SG293 defines visibility requirements within temporal scope as a business constraint on child actor access.

payload.description: Historical data must remain accessible
payload.justification: Signal SG297 mandates accessibility of historical data as a business constraint on data retention policy.

payload.description: Child user task collection access pattern rule
payload.justification: Data visibility requirements define logical access pattern rules governing how child users interact with task collections.

payload.description: Aggregation and reporting of earnings (weekly amounts and completion-based) for parent tracking
payload.justification: Temporal aggregation logic represents a logical function that computes and presents weekly earned amounts to children. | Logical reporting interface represents a function providing aggregated view of child earnings and task completion data to parents.

payload.description: Task completion event to child entity association
payload.justification: Signal explicitly describes logical association rule linking task completion events to child entities, directly representing a relationship between these logical data elements.

payload.description: Weekly task availability restoration cycle
payload.justification: Periodic state reset logic defining weekly cycle behavior represents a temporal cycle for restoring task availability.

payload.description: Historical task record persistence and accessibility requirement
payload.justification: Data retention requirement mandating logical persistence represents a design constraint on historical task record accessibility.

payload.description: Operating system version compatibility constraint for deployment
payload.justification: Signal explicitly describes a platform compatibility constraint specifying concrete operating system versions. This is a technical constraint governing deployment decisions at the Builder level.

payload.description: Weekly earnings total access event for child actors
payload.justification: Signal SG542 establishes weekly operational schedule boundary for earned amount access; represents a detailed timing event specification.

payload.description: Weekly task availability reset cycle
payload.justification: Signal SG544 specifies weekly operational cycle timing for task availability reset; this is a detailed sequencing specification for recurring operations.

payload.description: iOS mobile platform deployment node
payload.justification: Signal SG545 specifies iOS as one of three named deployment platform locations; Rule 5 requires separate CCI per named platform. | Signal SG545 specifies Android as one of three named deployment platform locations; Rule 5 requires separate CCI per named platform.

payload.description: Windows desktop platform deployment node
payload.justification: Signal SG545 specifies Windows as one of three named deployment platform locations; Rule 5 requires separate CCI per named platform.

payload.description: Child user interface for task claiming operations
payload.justification: Signal SG539 identifies children as operational actors executing task claiming activities; at Row 5 this maps to a detailed user interface specification for child actors.

payload.description: Parent user interface for task configuration and value assignment
payload.justification: Signal SG540 identifies parents as operational actors responsible for task configuration and monetary value assignment; this represents a distinct detailed user interface specification.

payload.description: Parent user interface for report access and pocket money distribution
payload.justification: Signal SG543 defines parent operational role in accessing reports to execute distribution activities; represents a detailed interface specification distinct from configuration activities.

payload.description: Child actor task visibility must include available and completed tasks within weekly cycles
payload.justification: Signal SG541 defines operational visibility requirements for child actors; at Row 5 this becomes a detailed validation constraint on what data must be visible to child interfaces.

payload.title: High Level Description
payload.description: Section: High Level Description

payload.completeness_rule: This register SHALL contain the identifiers of ALL Segment elements present in the ledger.

payload.description: Expresses the fundamental purpose of enabling children to claim and track completed pocket money tasks weekly.
Articulates parental motivation to define compensated task assignments with associated monetary values.
Captures the purpose of providing children visibility into available and completed task inventory.
Identifies the motivational goal of enabling children to view their weekly earnings summary.
Establishes parental need to access child performance reporting for accurate compensation distribution purposes.
Indicates stakeholder requirement for longitudinal task history access supporting accountability or trend analysis.
Section header indicating the commencement of conceptual system description.
Establishes the core business concept of children claiming completed tasks for monetary reward tracking.
Defines the parent capability to specify task entities with associated monetary value attributes.
Specifies the relationship between completed task entities and child entities through linkage.
Defines child actor visibility requirements for both available and completed task entities within temporal scope.
Establishes weekly aggregation capability for earned monetary totals visible to child actors.
Defines parent reporting capability displaying child-task-earnings relationships to support payment fulfillment.
Specifies the cyclical business rule for task availability reset at weekly temporal boundaries.
Mandates retention and accessibility of historical task data entities across temporal periods.
Logical association rule defining how task completion events are linked to child entities.
Data visibility requirements specifying logical access patterns for child users to task collections.
Temporal aggregation logic defining weekly computation and presentation of earned amounts to children.
Logical reporting interface providing parents aggregated view of child earnings and task completion data.
Periodic state reset logic defining weekly cycle behavior for task availability restoration.
Data retention requirement mandating logical persistence and accessibility of historical task records.
Platform compatibility constraint specifying concrete operating system versions for deployment.
Identifies children as operational actors who execute task claiming activities throughout the week on a temporal basis.
Identifies parents as operational actors responsible for task configuration and monetary value assignment activities.
Defines operational visibility requirements for child actors viewing available and completed tasks within weekly cycles.
Establishes weekly operational schedule boundary when child actors can access earned amount totals.
Defines parent operational role in accessing reports to execute pocket money distribution activities.
Specifies weekly operational cycle timing when task availability resets for claiming operations.
Defines deployment platform locations specifying where the system operates across iOS, Android, and Windows environments.

payload.completeness_rule: This register SHALL contain the identifiers of ALL Signal elements present in the ledger.
